—Solomon informed Huram about the specific tasks to be done in the temple. The mysteries of true religion, unlike those of Gentile superstitions, did not seek concealment. Solomon tried to fill Huram with great and high thoughts of the God of Israel. We should not be afraid or ashamed to embrace every opportunity to speak of God and to impress others with a deep sense of the importance of his favor and service. Now that the people of Israel kept close to the law and worship of God, the neighboring nations were willing to be taught by them in true religion, as the Israelites had been willing in the days of their apostasy to be infected with the idolatries and superstitions of their neighbors. A wise and pious king is evidence of the Lord’s special love for his people. How great then was God's love to his believing people in giving his only-begotten Son to be their Prince and their Savior.
